name: Gambler
flavor: The card-sharp's discipline: a Joker who trusts the deck more than his own hands, having learned exactly which rolls can be nudged in his favor, which bets are worth doubling, and which are strictly for suckers.

features.name: Stacked Deck
features.description: You've learned to load the deck in your favor. A number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1), which you regain when you finish a long rest, you can do one of the following, no action required: choose the result of a Wild Card draw instead of rolling the d8; reroll a d20 you just rolled for your own attack roll, ability check, or saving throw and use the new result; or target one creature within 30 feet of you that just made an attack roll or saving throw, forcing it to succeed on a Charisma saving throw against your gambit DC or reroll that d20 and use the new result. These uses don't cost Mayhem and are separate from your Gambit's Rewrite the Draw option — the two don't share a pool.

meta.save: Cha save vs Gambit DC (forced reroll)

features.name: Double Down
features.description: You can bet on your own luck before you know how a roll lands. Once per turn, before making an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw (not a Parry roll), you can spend 1 Mayhem to roll a d6 and add it to the total. If that d6 comes up a 1, the bet turns on you: you take [[1d4]] psychic damage as the trick backfires, in addition to whatever the roll itself produces. You must decide to Double Down before the roll's outcome is known.
